name: review-fix
description: Review the current change (breadth then depth) and fix findings until no Blocker or Major findings remain.
rubric: The breadth review pass (05) was run against the current diff this iteration, not a prior one.
The depth review pass (06) was run with the breadth findings and Architecture Brief as input.
Zero findings of severity Blocker remain.
Zero findings of severity Major remain.
Every fix applied this iteration was itself re-reviewed, not just confirmed absent from the old findings list.
The validation matrix commands for all touched scopes pass.
fixPrompt: Each iteration: (1) Run the breadth review pass (05-REVIEW-BREADTH) over the current diff. (2) Run the depth review pass (06-REVIEW-DEPTH) with the breadth findings pasted in to avoid duplication; include the Architecture Brief if one exists. (3) If no Blocker or Major findings remain, the loop converges — Minor/Nit findings are reported, not iterated on. (4) Otherwise, fix the Blocker and Major findings per 04-IMPLEMENT, re-running the validation matrix for touched scopes, then start the next iteration. If a finding challenges an Architecture Brief decision, evaluate it via 07-FEEDBACK before changing the design.
guardrails: Findings are fixed, not argued away — unless a 07-FEEDBACK evaluation produces a 'decision holds' verdict with evidence.
Do not re-architect beyond what the findings require; scope creep restarts review from zero.
Reviews in later iterations must re-examine the fixes themselves, not just confirm the old findings are gone.
onExhausted: Stop. Report the remaining Blocker/Major findings, the fix attempted for each, and why it did not resolve. Recommend whether the change needs a human review or a return to the Architect stage.
